herb_side_effects.name: causes irritation
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis causes irritation due to its potent compounds, which can lead to skin redness and inflammation in some individuals. This irritation can range from mild to severe, depending on the concentration of the plant's extracts and the sensitivity of the user. People who are prone to skin allergies or have sensitive skin should exercise caution when using calendula products.

herb_side_effects.name: triggers allergies
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis triggers allergies in some individuals due to its potent compounds and volatile oils. People with sensitivities or allergies may experience symptoms such as hives, itching, or skin irritation after exposure to the plant. If you have allergies, it's essential to exercise caution when handling calendula officinalis or using its products.

herb_side_effects.name: induces diarrhea
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis induces diarrhea in some individuals due to its high concentration of saponins and iridoids, which can stimulate bowel movements and cause loose stools. This side effect can be particularly problematic for people with pre-existing gastrointestinal issues or those taking medications that affect digestive health. In most cases, the diarrhea associated with calendula officinalis is mild and temporary, resolving on its own once the plant is discontinued.

herb_side_effects.name: produces nausea
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis produces nausea in some individuals who consume it, particularly when taken in large or concentrated doses. This side effect may be due to the plant's high saponin content, which can cause gastrointestinal upset in sensitive individuals. In most cases, nausea associated with calendula use is mild and temporary, but those experiencing persistent or severe symptoms should consult with a healthcare professional.

herb_side_effects.name: leads to stomach pain
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis leads to stomach pain, which can be a result of consuming large quantities or using high concentrations of the plant's extracts. However, it's essential to note that stomach pain is not a common side effect, and most people can use calendula without any issues. Nonetheless, those who do experience stomach pain may find relief by reducing their intake or switching to a different preparation method.

herb_side_effects.name: triggers skin rashes
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis triggers skin rashes in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. This reaction can range from mild redness and irritation to more severe symptoms, such as itching and blistering. In rare cases, repeated exposure to calendula officinalis may exacerbate the skin rash, making it a concern for those who frequently use products containing this plant.

herb_side_effects.name: affects respiratory system
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis affects respiratory system by potentially causing irritation and inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate conditions such as asthma and bronchitis. This can lead to symptoms like wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, making breathing more difficult. In severe cases, the respiratory issues can be a serious side effect that requires medical attention.

herb_side_effects.name: induces urticaria
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis induces urticaria, which is characterized by itchy, raised red patches or hives that can appear anywhere on the body. This side effect is thought to be an allergic reaction to the plant's bioactive compounds, which can cause the release of histamine and other chemical mediators. In rare cases, urticaria caused by calendula officinalis can be severe and require medical attention.

herb_side_effects.name: leads to digestive issues
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis leads to digestive issues, potentially causing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea in some individuals. These adverse effects can be attributed to the plant's potential allergenic properties or the body's intolerance to its chemical compounds. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using calendula officinalis to minimize the risk of digestive issues.

herb_side_effects.name: produces excessive sweating
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis produces excessive sweating, which is a potential side effect of its use in herbal remedies. This excessive sweating may be due to the plant's ability to stimulate the body's circulatory system and promote a release of toxins. However, in some individuals, this side effect can be uncomfortable and may require caution when using calendula officinalis products.

herb_side_effects.name: fosters eye irritation
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis fosters eye irritation in some individuals due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and saponins, which can cause redness, itching, and inflammation in the eyes. When applied topically or ingested, these compounds can trigger allergic reactions or sensitivities, leading to uncomfortable symptoms. It is essential to handle calendula products with care and follow proper usage guidelines to minimize the risk of eye irritation.

herb_side_effects.name: causes dizziness
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis causes dizziness due to the potential interaction of its chemical compounds with the nervous system. This side effect is often temporary and may be alleviated by reducing the dosage or discontinuing use. However, individuals with pre-existing conditions or those taking medications should consult a healthcare professional before using calendula officinalis products.

herb_side_effects.name: triggers abdominal cramping
herb_side_effects.herb_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis triggers abdominal cramping, often resulting from an allergic reaction or sensitivity to the plant's compounds. In some individuals, consuming calendula or using products derived from the plant may cause gastrointestinal discomfort, including cramping and diarrhea. It is essential to note that these side effects are typically mild and temporary, but individuals with pre-existing gastrointestinal issues should exercise caution when using calendula.

plant_side_effects.name: she suffers from atopic dermatitis exacerbation
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis she suffers from atopic dermatitis exacerbation. However, it's essential to note that calendula is often used as a treatment for atopic dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. plant_side_effects.name: she experiences diarrhea

plant_side_effects.name: she causes contact dermatitis
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis she causes contact dermatitis, which is a type of skin inflammation that can be itchy, red, and painful. This skin reaction is often triggered by the plant's allergenic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, and can occur when handling the plant or using its products. If left untreated, contact dermatitis can lead to further complications, including infection and scarring.

plant_side_effects.name: she causes fever

plant_side_effects.name: she experiences anticholinergic toxicity
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Calendula officinalis she experiences anticholinergic toxicity when the plant's extracts are consumed in large amounts, potentially leading to a range of symptoms such as confusion, agitation, and hallucinations.
